text: The Study of Philosophy of Ethics and Prophet’s Hadith as Education Tools in Realizing Humanistic, Just and Transformative Education Nur Fitrianti , 1Edwar Arief 2 Abstract—This study aims to apply Philosophy of Ethics and Prophet’s Hadith to educate students in accordance with the principles of humanistic, just and transformative education that respects humanity, care and the environment. The application of philosophy of ethics, according to Muhammad Abduho, has many philosophical values that apply to all aspects of human life as stated by Fahd El Sheiky in that there are two schools of thought of philosophy of ethics in human life which is moral ethics that educate students on the basis of reason, human values and societal and Prophet’s Hadith, on the other hand, is a Islamic value system that refers to the concept and values as enshrined in the Holy Qur’an and the Hadith. By combining philosophy of ethics and Prophet’s Hadith it can be inferred that Islamic education is very prospective in developing human personality through the aspects of being highly moral, highly sensitive to society, and spiritually and intellectually intelligent. In this study the researchers are focusing on several problems which are analyzed through qualitative literature study and content analysis on philosophical of ethics and prophet’s Hadith for educational purposes. From the result of the study, the relations between Philosophy of Ethics and Prophet’s Hadith are good, true, moral and humanitarian. According to the sources of references it is concluded that both of them come from the same basic principle. While between philosophy of ethics in which all things belong to society, humanity and other logics of goodness that correspond to each aspect and values and are part of the human and society structure. And Prophet’s Hadith comes from Islamic verses which have reference on the Holy Qur’an and the Hadith. Furthermore, as the findings have suggested, that all Islamic educational systems aim to build a humanistic education system through boosting the respect and sanctity of humanity in general, and that it aspires to establish a just educational system in which it makes sure that every student has an equal opportunity to learn, and it forbids any type of discrimination or humiliation for some students, and finally that all Islamic educational systems aspire to provide a revolutionary and activist education which turns the students into active change-makers in their society. Key Words—Philosophy of Ethics, Prophet’s Hadith, Humanistic Education, Just Education, and Revolutionary Education
